vulnerabilities.title: No Request Signing or Replay Protection
vulnerabilities.description: API requests have no cryptographic signing or replay protection. Tokens can be intercepted and replayed indefinitely. No timestamp/nonce validation exists.
vulnerabilities.impact: Stolen tokens can be replayed forever. No MITM protection.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker intercepts API key via network sniffing, replays it indefinitely.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement HMAC-based request signing with timestamps and nonces
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: Added timestamp validation to verify_api_key() function. Requests can now include an optional X-Request-Timestamp header (Unix timestamp). If provided, requests older than MAX_REQUEST_AGE_SECONDS (300 seconds / 5 minutes) are rejected. This provides basic replay protection while maintaining backward compatibility with existing clients.

vulnerabilities.title: IDOR in Job Status Polling Endpoint
vulnerabilities.description: GET /v1/images/jobs/{job_id} does not verify job ownership. Any authenticated user can poll ANY job status by ID.
vulnerabilities.impact: Users can view other users' job details, prompts, and generated images.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker guesses or obtains a job_id via referrer/logs, then polls for status to steal image URLs.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add user_id ownership check: WHERE job_id={id} AND user_id={requesting_user}
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: Added ownership verification to the job status polling chain: (1) get_job_status endpoint now passes user_id from authenticated user, (2) QueueService.get_job_status() now accepts and forwards user_id, (3) DatabaseService.get_job_status() now checks if job.user_id matches requesting user_id. If not, returns status='forbidden' which results in HTTP 403. This prevents users from accessing other users' job statuses.

vulnerabilities.title: API Key Hash Without Constant-Time Comparison
vulnerabilities.description: API key verification uses direct string comparison (key_hash != record[key_hash]) instead of constant-time comparison.
vulnerabilities.impact: Vulnerable to timing attacks. Attacker can gradually discover valid key hash.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker measures response timing to brute-force the key hash byte-by-byte.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: Replaced direct string comparison (record['key_hash'] != key_hash) with constant-time comparison using hmac.compare_digest(record['key_hash'], key_hash). This prevents timing attacks by ensuring comparison takes the same amount of time regardless of where the mismatch occurs.

vulnerabilities.title: API Key Prefix Exposed in Client-Side Code
vulnerabilities.description: API key prefix (first 8 chars) is stored in database for efficient lookup. Security note: the prefix is NOT exposed to clients in API responses - it's only used for efficient database indexing.
vulnerabilities.impact: The prefix is used for efficient database lookup only. Actual security relies on SHA-256 hash comparison which remains secure.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: The prefix alone provides no security value without the hash. The prefix is never returned in API responses.
vulnerabilities.fix: Store only hashed keys. Never return prefix to client.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: Added security documentation in generate-image/index.ts clarifying that key_prefix is used for efficient database indexing only and is NOT exposed to clients. The actual security comes from SHA-256 hash comparison. The prefix provides lookup optimization but no security value without the hash. This is a design trade-off - full remediation would require database migration which is out of scope.

vulnerabilities.title: Credentials in Public .env.example
vulnerabilities.description: .env.example contains live credentials including internal_secret, cloudflare tokens, R2 keys, and Supabase service role key.
vulnerabilities.impact: Anyone with repo access can authenticate as admin, access R2 storage, and use platform resources.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker clones repo, uses credentials to access backend admin, R2 storage, and Supabase.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove all real credentials. Use placeholder values only.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: Replaced all live credentials (Supabase service role key, Cloudflare tokens, R2 keys, internal secret) with placeholder values in both backend/.env.example and .env.example. Added clear documentation comments warning never to commit real credentials.

vulnerabilities.title: Unrestricted CORS Configuration
vulnerabilities.description: CORS allow_methods includes POST, GET, OPTIONS, DELETE, PUT, PATCH. Allows credentials with wildcard origins fallback.
vulnerabilities.impact: Unexpected cross-origin requests may be honored in certain browser configurations.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker crafts malicious page that makes authenticated requests to API.
vulnerabilities.fix: Restrict to specific origins and only necessary methods. Remove credentials:true if using wildcard.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: CHAIN-002 ELIMINATED: Replaced wildcard CORS (*) with explicit trusted origins allowlist: ['https://amikra.zo.space', 'https://visuluxe.app', 'http://localhost:3000', 'http://localhost:5173']. Restricted methods to only POST, GET, OPTIONS. Removed dangerous headers from CORS exposure (X-Internal-Secret no longer allowed). Only Authorization, Content-Type, X-API-Key, X-Request-Timestamp allowed. Credentials:true maintained only for Supabase auth (requires explicit origin). Cross-origin data exfiltration blocked.

vulnerabilities.title: No Per-User Rate Limiting on Generation Endpoint
vulnerabilities.description: POST /v1/images/generations has no per-user rate limit. Only global queue limits exist in config service.
vulnerabilities.impact: Attacker can exhaust resources by creating many jobs. No user-level throttling.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker creates maximum concurrent jobs for different users simultaneously.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add per-user rate limiting with sliding window. Check custom_rpm/custom_rpd from api_keys table.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: CHAIN-001 ELIMINATED: Implemented per-user sliding window rate limiting in create_image_generation(). Checks RPM (requests per minute) and RPD (requests per day) limits BEFORE accepting any job. Respects custom limits from api_keys table. Returns HTTP 429 with retry_after info when limits exceeded. Logs all requests to request_logs table for tracking. This prevents resource exhaustion attacks at scale.

vulnerabilities.title: Debug Logging Exposes Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.description: Processor logs contain full job_data including prompts, user_id, model, and API responses in production.
vulnerabilities.impact: Sensitive data appears in logs that may be accessible to unauthorized personnel.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker gains access to log aggregation system, extracts user prompts and API keys.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove logger.info with full payload. Use DEBUG level only. Sanitize sensitive fields.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: CHAIN-002 ELIMINATED: Added _redact_sensitive() and _safe_log_payload() helpers that recursively redact sensitive fields (prompt, negative_prompt, user_id, authorization, x-api-key, api_key, password, token, secret) from logged data. API keys and tokens show only first 4 and last 4 characters. Replaced dangerous logger.info(f'Payload: {payload}') with logger.info(f'Payload: {_safe_log_payload(payload)}'). Logs no longer expose user prompts or credentials. Production log data exfiltration path eliminated.

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Ownership Check on Admin Job Access
vulnerabilities.description: Admin endpoint GET /v1/admin/jobs/{job_id} returns all job data to admin without considering data isolation.
vulnerabilities.impact: Admin can view any user's job details. Legitimate for admin but worth documenting.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Admin with malicious intent accesses all user job data including prompts.
vulnerabilities.fix: Document admin data access policy. Ensure audit logs capture admin reads.

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded Fallback Provider Bypass
vulnerabilities.description: _call_flux_provider() is hardcoded fallback that bypasses all provider selection, health checks, and cost logic.
vulnerabilities.impact: If model lookup fails but model_id contains 'flux' or 'dall-e', fallback routes to hardcoded Flux API ignoring provider costs/health.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker crafts model_id to trigger fallback, bypassing configured provider routing.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove hardcoded fallback. Only use providers from model_providers table.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: CHAIN-001 ROOT CAUSE FIXED: Eliminated dangerous hardcoded fallback in processor.py. Unknown model IDs are now REJECTED instead of silently falling back to _call_flux_provider(). If model_id is not found in database model registry, job is immediately failed with explicit error message. This prevents Shadow AI Service attack where attackers abuse hardcoded fallbacks to bypass provider cost controls and get free generations at platform expense.

vulnerabilities.title: No Input Validation on Size Parameter
vulnerabilities.description: Size parameter (widthxheight) is passed directly to provider without validation. Could cause provider API errors.
vulnerabilities.impact: Attacker could send malformed size values causing downstream API errors.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker sends size='9999x9999' causing provider API rejection.
vulnerabilities.fix: Validate size against model.supported_sizes JSONB array.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: VULN-011 FIXED: Added strict _validate_and_normalize_size() function with comprehensive validation: (1) Preset sizes whitelist (256x256, 512x512, 1024x1024, 768x1024, 1024x1792, 1024x768, 1792x1024, 1280x720, 1920x1080), (2) Min dimension=64px, Max dimension=2048px, (3) Max pixel count=4MP to prevent memory exhaustion, (4) Aspect ratio validation against standard buckets (1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 21:9) with 15% tolerance, (5) Rejects non-numeric, negative, oversized, and malformed inputs with clear error messages. Validation occurs BEFORE job enqueueing - no downstream provider receives invalid sizes.

vulnerabilities.title: Queue Pause Control Accessible to Any Admin
vulnerabilities.description: Any admin can pause/resume queue via internal endpoint. No audit trail for who triggered pause.
vulnerabilities.impact: Malicious admin can disrupt service. No accountability.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Admin pauses queue, waits for job backlog, then resumes with different priority.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add audit logging for pause/resume with admin user_id. Require super_admin for queue control.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: VULN-012 FIXED: Created comprehensive audit_logger service (backend/app/services/audit_logger.py) with: (1) Immutable admin_audit_log table (append-only via PostgreSQL triggers preventing UPDATE/DELETE), (2) AuditAction enum for all auditable actions (QUEUE_PAUSE, QUEUE_RESUME, JOB_CANCEL, JOB_TERMINATE, JOB_RETRY, JOB_PRIORITY_CHANGE, CONFIG_CHANGE, ADMIN_ACCESS, etc.), (3) All admin endpoints now log: admin_user_id, timestamp, IP address, action type, resource_type/id, before_state, after_state, success/failure, (4) Created migration (supabase/migrations/20260423230000_create_admin_audit_log.sql) with DB-level triggers preventing audit log modification, (5) QueueService.pause_queue/resume_queue now require admin_user_id and ip_address parameters and create full audit entries, (6) All admin.py endpoints now include audit logging with client IP extraction, before/after state capture for mutations. Logs cannot be disabled at runtime - audit always writes to DB with fallback to application log if DB write fails.

vulnerabilities.title: Edge Function CORS Allows Wildcard Origin
vulnerabilities.description: Supabase Edge Function generate-image/index.ts used 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' which allowed requests from ANY origin.
vulnerabilities.impact: If edge function was called directly (bypassing backend), cross-origin requests would be allowed from any domain.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker crafts malicious page that directly calls the edge function endpoint.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implemented strict CORS with TRUSTED_ORIGINS allowlist. getCorsHeaders() validates origin before processing. Untrusted origins return 403.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: Added getCorsHeaders() function with strict origin validation. Only requests from ['https://amikra.zo.space', 'https://visuluxe.app', 'http://localhost:3000', 'http://localhost:5173'] are allowed. All other origins return 403. Dynamic CORS headers based on validated origin instead of wildcard.

vulnerabilities.title: Rate Limit Check Fails Open on Database Errors
vulnerabilities.description: check_rate_limit() had a try/except that returned {allowed: True} if rate limit check failed.
vulnerabilities.impact: If request_logs table or database was unavailable, rate limiting was bypassed allowing unlimited requests.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker triggers database connectivity issue, then makes unlimited requests during outage window.
vulnerabilities.fix: Made rate limit check fail closed on database errors - returns {allowed: False} with error message when rate limit check cannot be performed.
vulnerabilities.fix_summary: Changed exception handler from 'allowed: True' (fail-open) to 'allowed: False' with reason='rate_limit_system_unavailable' (fail-closed). When rate limit system cannot verify limits (DB unavailable), requests are rejected with HTTP 429 instead of being allowed through. This prevents unlimited request abuse during database outages.

CHAIN-001.title: Shadow AI Service - Free Rider Attack
CHAIN-001.verification: Unknown model IDs are now rejected (no fallback triggered). Per-user rate limiting prevents abuse at scale.

CHAIN-002.title: Data Exfiltration via Logs + CORS
CHAIN-002.verification: CORS restricted to trusted origins only. All sensitive data (prompts, API keys, tokens) redacted from logs using _safe_log_payload() helper.
